detailed_analysis: The file is structurally clean: 10 focused tests, no shared mutable state, clear AAA sections, and three small helpers that keep arrange blocks readable. There are no sleeps, no real subprocesses, no network, and no test interdependencies. However, the suite suffers from several recurring test-quality smells that weaken the contract it actually pins on the MCP-server sync feature. (1) Lazy Tests / Partial Contract: many tests touch the code path but only assert a fraction of the observable contract. test_mcp_adopt_between_synthetic_adapters verifies key-set membership but does not check that pre-existing servers' commands survived. test_mcp_pair_id_injected_into_slot asserts only that pair_id is truthy and equal across slots — not the pair_id format, length, or that it appears nowhere it shouldn't. test_mcp_remove_slot_removes_in_other_tool asserts archive_dir.exists() but never opens the archive. (2) Tests that pass when the SUT is broken: test_mcp_secret_policy_refuse_blocks_adoption asserts changed == 0 and not beta_file.exists() — but any failure to write beta would also satisfy these. No positive signal that the refusal-path was exercised. (3) Sensitive Equality: test_mcp_secret_policy_permissive_warns uses substring matching against a log message. test_mcp_secret_policy_redact_rewrites pins the placeholder format '${env:AGENTS_SYNC_REDACTED_1}' as a magic string. (4) Conditional Test Logic in a Helper: _pair_id_for_slot contains a for loop, an any(...) predicate, and a raise AssertionError fallback. Used by four tests. (5) Eager / Multi-Aspect Tests: test_mcp_archive_is_per_slot asserts archive entry count, archive content shape, absence of mcpServers envelope, absence of filesystem from archive, and that the unrelated filesystem server is intact in beta — two distinct contracts (archive-per-slot AND no-spurious-mutation) in one test. (6) Wrong Layer of Test: test_mcp_two_adapters_same_shared_file_different_slots_no_collision calls syncer.discovery.discover() and syncer.discovery.block_target_collisions() directly — internal subsystems, not the public sync_once boundary. (7) Missing critical contracts: no idempotency test, no key-ordering test on mcpServers, and no head-on conflict-resolution test. (8) Fixture-Hidden State: _baseline_config injects eleven 'unused-*' directory paths that exist solely to satisfy Syncer's config schema. The dominant pattern is under-pinning.

findings.variant: Lazy Test / Tests Pass When SUT Is Broken
findings.problem: All four assertions are negative (nothing happened). The test cannot distinguish 'refuse policy correctly blocked adoption' from 'sync_once silently bailed out for an unrelated reason'. There is no positive signal.

Test claims to verify 'same-slot collision' behaviour but asserts only the absence of side effects. If the collision detector were removed entirely and sync_once happened to no-op for the shared-file case, this test would still pass.
Test couples to free-text log wording. Any rephrasing breaks the test without indicating a behavioural regression.
The placeholder string format is duplicated as a literal. If the redaction template changes, this test will fail without any user-visible regression.
Helper contains a for-loop, an any(...) predicate, and a fallback raise. Used by four tests, so a single helper bug masks four regressions.
Two distinct contracts in one test: (a) archive is per-slot, and (b) unrelated filesystem server is left intact in beta.
Bypasses the sync_once public boundary that every other test uses. Couples the test to internal subsystem composition. Its sibling uses sync_once — the pair is inconsistent.
Only checks pair_id truthiness and cross-slot equality. Does not pin format/length, absence of leakage into siblings, stability across a second sync_once, or correspondence with state.json.

findings.variant: Lazy Test — Archive Content Not Verified
findings.problem: Asserts that the archive directory exists after removal but never opens it. If removal-propagation regressed to 'create empty archive dir and delete state', this test would still pass.

findings.variant: Obscure Test / Mystery Guest in Helper
findings.problem: Eleven 'unused-*' directory keys exist solely to satisfy Syncer's config schema. They obscure the four keys that actually drive the MCP-server contract.

findings.variant: Missing Contract — No Idempotency / No Conflict-Resolution / No Key-Ordering Test
findings.problem: Three load-bearing contracts of a sync engine are unpinned: idempotency, conflict resolution between two concurrently-edited slots, and key-order preservation.
Two raw numeric mtimes (1000, 2000) encode the reconciliation rule 'newer mtime wins' implicitly. The test name says 'by_slot_key' but the actual mechanism is mtime-based recency arbitration.

verdict_summary: Structurally clean test file (no flakiness, no shared state, clear AAA), but under-pins the MCP-server sync contract: several tests assert only negative side effects, key invariants (idempotency, conflict resolution, key ordering) are unpinned, and two tests couple to log wording / placeholder format rather than structured signals.
